steps.title: Read the intercept
steps.content: The line crosses the $y$-axis at $(0,-1)$, so the equation has $y$-intercept $-1$.

steps.title: Find the slope
steps.content:
Using the points $(0,-1)$ and $(4,-2)$, the slope is
$$
m=\frac{-2-(-1)}{4-0}=\frac{-1}{4}=-\frac{1}{4}
$$

steps.title: Write the equation
steps.content: In slope-intercept form, $$ y=-\frac{1}{4}x-1 $$ The answer is A.
